def: s.m. = padārth, q.v.s.v. pad.
s.m. See s.v. pad.
s.f. Inclination to fart, flatulency.
adj. Inclined to fart, flatulent; addicted to farting ( = padoṛā).
s.m. Reward, recompense, compensation, satisfaction, requital, retribution, retaliation, revenge.
s.m. Reward, recompense, compensation, satisfaction, requital, retribution, retaliation, revenge.
s.m. Footman, pedestrian; peon; foot-soldier; a pawn (in chess).
s.m. Footman, pedestrian; peon; foot-soldier; a pawn (in chess).
s.m. = padvī, q.v.

s.f. Roadway, path; line, row, range; the designation of a class of literary compositions, a guide, ritual, manual, a treatise on any particular science; a work on any act or ceremony, detailing the mode of its performance and collecting the texts connected with it; manner, custom; title, surname, family name ( = padwī).

s.f. Roadway, path; line, row, range; the designation of a class of literary compositions, a guide, ritual, manual, a treatise on any particular science; a work on any act or ceremony, detailing the mode of its performance and collecting the texts connected with it; manner, custom; title, surname, family name ( = padwī).
s.f. Way, path, alley.
adj. & s.m. Addicted to farting; — a farter; (met.) one easily frightened, a coward ( = pādū; padakkaṛ).
s.m. Tutor, teacher, schoolmaster; priest.
s.m. = pādhā, q.v.
s.m. = pāṛhā, q.v.
s.m. = pradhān, q.v.
v.n. To go or come, proceed, depart; set out; to arrive; to sit, sit down, take a seat.
s.f. = pāṛhī, q.v.
v.n. = padhārnā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Having feet, footed; entitled to a fourth part or share; — a footed creature; a footed aquatic animal, an amphibious animal.
s.f. (local), Rice in the husk, paddy.
s.f. The tonnage or measurement of boats or ships.
adj. Open, public, clear, evident, visible, manifest, conspicuous.
adj. & s.m. Going on foot; (in Pros.) one foot long, containing only one division; — footman, pedestrian; foot-soldier; the point of the foot
s.m. = pradīp, q.v.
s.f. 'The lotus-hued one,' an epithet of Lakshmī, and of Śrī, the goddess of fortune and wife of Vishnu; the plant Clerodendrum siphonanthus; the plant Hibiscus mutabilis.

s.m. The lotus-flower, Nelumbium speciosum (often confounded with the water-lily or Nymphæa alba); a lotus-like ornament; a particular mark or mole on the human body; a spot; red or coloured marks on the face and trunk of an elephant; an army arrayed in the form of a lotus; a particular posture of the body in religious meditation; one of the personified treasures of Kuvera; name of a Nāga; a species of fragrant plant; a particular high number, ten billions (according to the Śāstras), or one thousand billions (according to the Dasturuʼl-ʻamal or royal ordinances of Akbar)
subs: (padma+āsan), s.m. A lotus-seat, seat or throne in the shape of a lotus (esp. one on which idols are placed); a particular posture in religious meditation (sitting with the thighs crossed with one hand resting on the left thigh, the other held up with the thumb upon the heart, and the eyes directed to the tip of the nose)
(padma+āk˚), s.m. Lotus-pool, a large deep tank or pond abounding in lotuses
(padma+āl˚), s.f. 'Whose dwelling is in the lotus,' an epithet of Lakshmī
s.m. The petal of a lotus; — Costus speciosus
s.m. Name of a purāṇa describing the period during which the world was a lotus
s.m. The plant Pterospermum acerifolium, or Webera corymbosa
s.f. The small tree Hibiscus mutabilis
s.m. Resin of the Pinus longifolia
s.m. Name of a fragrant wood used in medicine (and described as cooling and tonic)
s.m. 'The lotus-born,' an epithet of Brahma
s.f. A species of prickly nightshade, Alhagi maurorum
(padma+ut˚), s.m. The plant Carthamus tinctorius, safflower

def: s.m. An army arrayed in the form of a lotus-flower; the tree Costus speciosus, or C. Arabicus, and its wood; a kind of leprosy.
s.m. The Bhoj-patra or birch-tree (the bark of which is used for writing on, wrapping up shawls, &c.).
s.m. See s.v. padma.

r: padmāvatī, lit, Padmāvatī, Lakshmī, Jarat kāru, Kāsim-bāzār
def: s.f. lit. 'Abounding in lotus-flowers'; name of a celebrated princess (whose adventures are related in a romance entitled Padmāvatī); an epithet of Lakshmī, the goddess of wealth; name of the wife of the sage Jarat kāru; name of the main stream of the Ganges between the Kāsim-bāzār river and the sea.

s.f. A lotus, Nelumbium speciosum; a multitude of lotuses, or a lake abounding in them; a particular magical art, witch-craft; a woman of the first and most excellent of the four classes into which the sex is distinguished: (The four classes are, Padminī, Ćītriṇī, Sankhinī, and Hastinī).
(padma+uttara), s.m. see s.v. padmā.
v.n. To break wind, to fart; — to acknowledge one's inferiority; to succumb; to turn tail.
adj. & s.m. Addicted to farting; — a farter; (met.) one easily frightened, a coward ( = pādū; padakkaṛ).
s.m. See s.v. pāda.
(pada+udaka), s.m. see s.v. pad.
adj. & s.m. Addicted to farting; — a farter; (met.) one easily frightened, a coward ( = pādū; padakkaṛ).
s.m. An insect which constructs a hole in the ground as a trap for ants on which it feeds; the ant-lion.
s.m. Christian priest, clergyman, minister, chaplain, missionary.
s.m. Leave, dismissal, farewell
s.m. King, &c. = bādshāh, q.v.
contrac. of pādshāh, q.v.
adj. & adv. = bādshāhāna, q.v.
s.f. = bādshāhat, q.v.
adj. & s.f. = bādshāhī, q.v.
s.f. Shoe, slipper.
adj. & s.m. Addicted to farting; — one who farts, farter ( = paddū, padoṛā).
adj. & s.m. Having feet; going with feet; — any creature that goes on feet.
s.f. Wooden shoe; shoe, slipper; sandal.
s.f. Road, way, path; situation, site, place; station, rank, status, dignity, character; title, degree, titular name, surname, patronymic
s.m. Traveller, wayfarer.
s.m. Water for washing the feet
subs: s.m. A metal vessel for washing the feet.

def: s.m. Metre; verse, poetry.
s.m. Antidote, Bezoar stone.
s.m. Foot, &c. = pā, q.v.
subs: s.m. The royal residence, the capital
v.n. To settle accounts; to call to account
s.m. A necessary, a privy; — pāʼe-ḵẖānā phirnā, v.n. To visit the necessary, to go to the rear
adj. lit. 'Having feet'; lasting, durable, permanent, strong
s.m. Snare, noose, trap, gin, springe
adj. Chained by the leg, fettered; encumbered (with a family, &c.)
s.m. Loop or slip-knot tied to the legs of falcons; a staple
s.m. A person who purchases goods from the manufacturer to sell to the merchant; agent; broker; a retail dealer; hawker, pedlar
adj. Beating the feet in dancing, stamping with the feet
s.m. Rank, dignity; a stable. (N.B. Other compounds will be found under the contrac. form pā, q.v.).

def: s.m. A leg of a pair of trousers or drawers; short trousers, drawers, breeches.
s.m. See s.v. pāʼě.
s.m. = pāṅʼića; pāʼeća, q.v.
subs: v.n. lit. 'To make the drawers heavy'; (in the language of women) to make a vow of rigid seclusion, to vow not to go out any more.

def: s.f. Foot or bottom of a bed ( = pāṅyatī).
s.m. Bezoar stone ( = pā-zahr; pād-zahr, q.v.).
s.f. Syrup.
s.f. (Prov.), A turban ( = pagṛī); — pāg, pāg-tāg, s.m. Poll-tax formerly levied on all males over twelve years of age.
s.m. The foot; step; — pag uṭhānā; v.n. To give over running
subs: v.n. To beat time with the foot
s.f. The sole of the foot
v.n. To go, depart, leave; to travel; — pag-ḍanḍī, s.f. A by-path, by-way, foot-path, track; — pag-ḍanḍī lenā, v.n. To take the footpath; to track, trace.

def: s.m. = paghā, q.v.; — also perf. part. of pagnā, q.v.
s.m. A troop of horse among Marhaṭṭas); a stud.
adj. (Prov.) Ripened, ripe; come to a head.
s.f. Dawn of day, morning twilight
adj. & s.m. Mad, insane; foolish, silly; — madman, idiot, fool ( = paglā; bāʼolā)
ex: اُن سے دُور رَہو، وہ لوگ پاگَل ہَیں
un se dūr raho, vo log pāgal ha͠i
Stay away from them, they're insane

def: s.m. Madness, insanity; foolishness, folly.
s.m. Madness, insanity; foolishness, folly.
s.m. A kind of drum or tabor.
s.m. Enclosure round a field, trench, mound, bank.
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese pagar. In Pakistan, borrowed from Gujarati પગાર (pagār).

def: s.m. Wet earth or clay for building a wall or plastering (syn. gārā).
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese pagar. In Pakistan, borrowed from Gujarati પગાર (pagār).

def: s.m. A rope fastened round the neck or foot of a bullock, a tether; the rope which passes under the tail of a bullock and is connected with the harness on the back.
s.m. A trader in cloth, iron, &c.
s.m. A kind of hard iron or steel; metal.
s.m. = paghaiyā, q.v.

ety: pāg, q.v.+iyā = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. A turban, &c. = pagṛī, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Mad; foolish, &c. = pāgal, q.v. and cf. pagnā.
v.n. (Prov.) To ripen: to come to a head ( = paknā).
v.n. To be dipped or soaked in syrup; (met.) to be infatuated (with), be enamoured (of), be madly in love (with).
v.t. To dip in or cover with syrup; to form a cake of crystallized sugar.
s.f. A turban; a mark or token of distinction, (hence) honour, distinction, respect, dignity; head, poll; a kind of poll-tax
subs: (-kī), To disgrace, dishonour; to overreach, take in, cheat
v.n. To be obstinate, to persist or persevere
v.n. To put on a turban; to become a person of consequence; to be a maker of turbans
v.n. To be presented with, or to put on, a turban (as a token of honour, headship, or hereditary succession)
(-kī), 'To snatch off the turban (of)'; to overreach, practice deceit (on), to act treacherously (towards).

def: s.m. Chewing the cud, rumination (syn. jugālī).
v.n. To chew the cud, to ruminate.
v.n. = pāgurānā, q.v.
s.f. A kind of stone (which is ground along with alum and cloves and mixed with opium to form a lep or poultice for sore eyes).
s.f. = poh, q.v.
s.f. The ceremony of initiation into the Sikh religion and community.
s.m. A flock (of cotton, wool, &c.).
s.m. A side ( = pahlū, q.v.).

ety: Prk. पढइल्लो or पढमिल्लो = S. प्रथमः+इलः
def: s.m. Beginning, commencement; initiative; aggression; — adv. In the first place
subs: (meṅ), To make a beginning; to take the initiative (in).

def: s.m. A stone.
s.m. = pāhān, q.v.

s.m. Mountain, hill; rock; anything very large or huge, or hard, or strong, or firm; anything very tedious, burdensome or heavy, &c. (hence the word may often be conveniently rendered adjectively by great, large, huge, tall, stupendous; burdensome, heavy, tedious, &c.)
subs: s.f. lit. 'Mountain-like nights,' long and tedious nights (esp. of sorrow)
s.f. One of the seven sisters who are regarded as goddesses of small-pox, measles, chicken-pox, &c.

def: s.m. A division of time consisting of eight ghaṛīs or three hours, an eighth part of a day, a watch
subs: s.m. A watch of the day, a day's watch; the first watch of the day (from 6 to 9 A.M.): pahar-rāt, s.m. A watch of the night; the first watch of the night (from 6 to 9 P.M.).

def: s.m. The multiplication table.
s.f. A small hill a hillock.
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to the hills; hilly, mountainous; — a mountaineer, hill-man
s.m. A watch man, &c. = pahrā, q.v. (the watchman of a village is commonly employed as a servant and messenger).
adj. & s.m. = pahāṛī, q.v.
s.m. The Indian mulberry tree, Moras indica.

s.f. Knowledge, acquaintance, ascertainment, recognition, experience, discrimination, discernment; distinguishing mark, characteristic; indication, token, sign (also written paićhān); — an acquaintance ( = jān-pahćān).
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi पहिचान (pahicāna), from Sanskrit प्रत्यभिज्ञान (pratyabhijñāna). Related to پہچاننا.

def: v.t. To perceive, know, comprehend, understand; to recognize, identify; to distinguish, discern, discriminate
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀘𑁆𑀘𑀳𑀺𑀬𑀸𑀡𑀇 (paccahiyāṇaï), from Sanskrit प्रत्यभिजानाति (pratyabhijānāti).

def: v.t. (prov.) = pahćānnā, q.v.
adv. = pahle; and s.f. = pahelī, qq.v.
s.f. A riddle, enigma.
v.t. (prov.) = pahinnā, q.v.
s.m. Non-resident cultivator; temporary occupant of village land; tenant at will
subs: s.f. Fallow land; — pāʼe-kār, s.m. = pāʼe-kāsht
pāʼe-kāsht, s.m. Non-resident cultivator; cultivation by non-residents.

def: adj. = pahilauṭā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. First-born; &c. = palauṭhā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. First-born; &c. = palauṭhā, q.v.

ety: the preceding+Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं
def: s.m. Primogeniture.
v.t. See پہننا pahinnā.

ety: fr. the trans. pahinānā; — pahinā˚ or pahināw˚ or pihnāw˚ = Prk. पिनहाव(इ) or पिनहावे(इ) = S. पिनाहय(ति), caus. of पिनह; — cf. pahirnā and pahirānā
def: v.t. To put on, to wear (clothes, shoes, &c.); to dress; — s.m. Clothing, dress.
wety: From a metathesis of Old Hindi पिन्हना (pinhanā), from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *पिणहइ (piṇahaï), from Sanskrit पिनह्यति (pinahyati, “wears, ties on, fastens”). Cognate with Punjabi ਪਹਿਨਣਾ (pahinṇā).

ety: Prk. परिधावे(इ) or परिधाव(इ) or परिहाव(इ) = S. caus. परिधापय(ति), rt. धा with परि
def: s.m. Dress, &c. = pahnāwā, q.v.

ety: Prk. परिधावे(इ) or परिधाव(इ) or परिहाव(इ) = S. caus. परिधापय(ति), rt. धा with परि
def: s.m. Dress, &c. = pahnāwā, q.v.
s.f. Dress, &c. = pahnāwā, q.v.; garments bestowed guests at a wedding ( = pahnāwān, and next).
s.f. Dresses given to guests at a wedding; — a woman who dresses the guests at a wedding, a tire-woman.

ety: pahir˚ = Prk. परिधे(इ), or परिध(इ) = S. परिधीय(ते), rt. धा with परि
def: v.n. To put on, to wear, &c. = pahinnā, q.v.: — s.m. Clothing.

ety: Prk. पहियओ or पहिअओ = S. पथिकः
def: s.m. Wheel (of a carriage, machine, &c.).
adj. & adv. (f. -ī), First, the first; former, pricr, previous, original, primary; leading, chief, main, principal; — previously, before; soon; rather
subs: pahile, adv. At first, first, in the first place; previously, already; hitherto; of old; sooner, before (governing the gen. in -ke, or the abl.); chiefly; rather (than, -se)
pahle-pahle, adv. First of all, in the first place or instance; of old; originally
adv. From the first, from the very commencement; at first, in the first place; already, beforehand
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa पहिल (pahila), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀳𑀺𑀮 (pahila), from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀧𑁆𑀭𑀣𑀺𑀮𑁆𑀮 (*prathilla). Sanskrit प्रथम (prathamá, “first”) was reanalyzed as having an ordinal suffix -म (-ma), which was replaced by Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀮𑁆𑀮- (-illa-). Also cognate to now-obsolete Classical Persian فَرْدُم (fardum, “first”). Doublet of فَرْدُم (fardum).

def: s.m. Name of the country where Pehlevī was spoken, ancient Persia; — a city; — a saint; — a hero; a wrestler ( = pahlvān).

s.m. Name of a people, the Persians; the country of the ancient Persians; — name of a degraded Kshatriya race conquered by Sagara and sentenced by him to wear beards (see Wilson's Vishṇu-Purāṇa, p. 375).
adj. & s.f. Of or pertaining to Pahlav, q.v.; of a city; of or pertaining to a hero; — the ancient language of Persia, the Pehlevi.
adv. See s.v. pahlā.

ety: Pehl. pahru; Z. pereśu; S. pārśva
def: s.m. A side; flank, wing; a facet; — utility; profit, advantage; indirect or crooked expedient, dishonourable or fraudulent means
subs: adv. On the side; on one side, laterally

s.f. Drawing aside, withdrawing (from); evasion, shirking, neglect; — pahlū-tihī karnā (-meṅ), To draw aside or apart, to draw back (from), to withdraw, retire, retreat; to decline, avoid; to evade, shirk
adj. Having sides, having facets; — profiting, benefiting, advantageous; indirect; ambiguous; specious, dissimulating; — scurrilous (language)
adj. & s.m. Sitting by the side; — a companion.

def: s.m. Hero; champion; athlete, wrestler, a stout and sturdy fellow.
s.f. Heroism; athletic exercise.
s.m. Width, breadth, extension, ampleness; — adj. Wide, &c. = pahnā, q.v.
s.m. = panhī, q.v.
adj. Broad, wide, ample, extensive, spacious
s.f. Width, breadth, spaciousness, ampleness, extensiveness.

ety: caus. of pahannā; the ā˚ of pahnā˚ = āw = Prk. āwe = S.āpi
def: v.t. To cause to be dressed; to cause to put on; to put on, to dress ( = pahirānā).

ety: rt. of pahinnā +āʼo or āw = Prk. āwe or āwa = S. आपि+ कः
def: s.m. Clothing, clothes, dress, garment.
s.m. = pahlav, q.v.

ety: rt. of pahinnā +āʼo or āw = Prk. āwe or āwa = S. आपि+ कः
def: s.m. Clothing, clothes, dress, garment.
s.f. Garments bestowed on guests and relations at a wedding ( = pahrāwan).
adj. See s.v. pahnā.
s.f. The plant Pistia stratioites.
s.m. A turn of watch; a charge; — a watch, watchman, sentinel; a guard, a corporal and six; — turn, time, period
subs: v.n. To change the guard, to relieve the watch or guard
(-par), To place a guard (over or at)
v.n. To keep watch, to mount guard
v.t. To give in charge to a watch or the guard; to place in custody
v.n. To be given in charge to a watch or the guard; to be given in custody
pahre-meṅ rakhnā, v.t. = pahre-meṅ biṭhānā, q.v.

def: s.m. = pahrā, q.v.
v.t. = pahnānā, q.v.
s.m. A watch man, &c. = pahrā, q.v. (the watchman of a village is commonly employed as a servant and messenger).
s.m. A watchman, sentinel, &c. = pahrā, q.v.
s.m. A watchman, sentinel, &c. = pahrā, q.v.
s.m. Person, individual; a contemptuous form of address.
v.n. = pahuṅćnā, q.v.
v.n. (Prov.) = pahǒṅćnā, q.v.
s.m. (Old H.) = pokhar, q.v.
s.f. (Old H.) = pr̤ithivī, q.v.
s.m. A guest, visitor.
s.m. = pāhun, q.v.; — son-in-law.
s.m. = pāhunā, q.v.
s.f. Hospitality, entertainment of guests or visitors.
s.f. = pahunaʼī, q.v.
s.f. Hospitality, entertainment, reception (of guests or visitors)
subs: (-kī), To practise hospitality, to entertain, act the host, to receive.

def: s.f. Arrival; reach; access, admittance; attainment, comprehension; sagacity, penetration; capacity, power; — receipt, acknowledgment.
s.m. The fore-arm, the wrist.

ety: caus. of pahuṅćnā; the ā˚ of pahuṅćā˚ = Prk. caus. aff. आवे or आव = S. आपि
def: v.t. To cause to arrive, cause to reach; to cause to be sent; to convey, escort, conduct, accompany, see (one) to (a place), to bring; to send, forward, transmit; to cause, occasion, bring to pass; to inflict (punishment, misery, &c.) upon
subs: v.t. To cause to arrive or to reach, to send, forward, transmit; to convey, conduct, &c.

def: v.t. (Braj) = pahǒṅćānā.
s.f. An ornament worn on the wrist, a bracelet (esp. a bracelet of gold beads strung together and fastened with a clasp).

ety: pahuṅć˚ = Prk. पहुच्च(इ) = S. पर्याप्नो(ति); or fr. S. प्र+भू; or उप+सञ्जर्
def: v.n. To arrive, reach, come (upon), come (to or up to); to accrue, extend, befall; to have access (to).
ex: ہَوَائی جَہاز دُبَئی تَک پَہُن٘چا ہَے۔
havāī jahāz dubaī tak pahũcā hai.
The airplane has reached as far as Dubai.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀳𑀼𑀘𑁆𑀘 (pahucca) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: v.n. = pahuṅćnā, q.v.
v.t. = pahǒṅćānā, q.v.
s.f. A female guest.
s.m. A flower = pushp, q.v.
v.n. To lie down, &c. = pauṛhnā, q.v.
s.f. See پائي pāʼī.
s.f. A small copper coin; the fourth part of an ānā or anna, a paisā or pice; a vertical line (in Hindī accounts) denoting the fourth part of an anna; the twelith part of an anna, a pie.
s.f. Pins used by weavers to wind off the warp from; a light walking cane or stick, a switch
subs: v.t. To brush or clean the warp after it has been stretched for weaving.

ety: contrac. of pāʼe, or S. पाद्य
def: s.m. The foot; footstep; track, trace, mark, vestige; pursuit; — nerve, tendon, sinew; — adv. In the footsteps (of, -ke), in the track (of), in pursuit (of), following; after, behind ( = pīćhe); by reason (of), on account (of), therefore
subs: pai-ba-pai, pai-dar-pai, adv. Step by step; one after another, in succession, successively, consecutively; repeatedly, continuously, incessantly
v.n. To have a splint (a horse)
s.m. A privy; cesspool; — filth, dirt ( = pāʼe ḵẖāna, q.v.)
pai-rau (see rav or rau) adj. & s.m. Following; — follower

s.f. Following, going after; pursuit; prosecution; effort, exertion, endeavour; following the example (of), imitation; adherence; observance, compliance; hunting (after), search, quest, investigation; consequence; precedent

(-kī), To go after, follow, pursue, trace, track, seek, hunt (for); to prosecute, conduct (a suit, &c.); to continue, persevere (in a course); to follow the example (of), to imitate; to observe, comply (with)
(-kā), To follow, pursue; to be importunate; to persecute, &c. ( = pīćhā karnā, q.v.)
pai-kaṛhā, s.m. pai-kaṛī, s.f. Iron chains (for the legs of convicts), fetters;-ornamental rings worn on the ankles, anklets
(-par), To trace, track, get scent (of), obtain a clue (to); to find out, discover; to comprehend.

def: s.m. Non-resident cultivator; temporary occupant of village land; tenant at will
subs: s.f. Fallow land; — pāʼe-kār, s.m. = pāʼe-kāsht
pāʼe-kāsht, s.m. Non-resident cultivator; cultivation by non-residents.

def: s.m. Turn, twist, &c. = peć, q.v.
s.f. = H پيچهاننا पैछानना paićhānnā, v.t. See pahćān and pahćānnā.
v.t. = H پيچهان पैछान paićhān, s.f. See pahćān and pahćānnā.

ety: Zend paiti+dayā, rt. dī; S. prati+rt. dhī
def: part. adj. Born, created, generated, produced; invented, discovered, manifested, manifest, exhibited; procured, acquired, earned, gained; — s.f. That which is earned, earnings, gain, profit, income; interest; emoluments; perquisites, bribes (syn. yāft)
subs: v.t. To bring into being, to create, make, form, produce, give rise to, occasion; to raise, bring forth, breed, hatch; to originate, invent, discover; to find; to procure, acquire, earn, gain, realize
v.n. To be born; to be created, &c.; to arise, spring up, be produced; to be found; to be earned, &c.

def: s.f. Birth; creation, production; origin, rise; produce; earnings, profits, &c. (see paidā); — one of the titles of the Book of Genesis.
adj. Natural; original; inborn, innate, &c. (syn. aṣlī; jibillī; ḵẖalqī).
adj. & adv. Walking; on foot; — s.m. Foot-soldier; infantry.
adv. See s.v. pai.
s.f. Produce (of land, &c.), yield; fruit; harvest; profits (of trade), proceeds, out-turn, income.
s.f. Produce (of land, &c.), yield; fruit; harvest; profits (of trade), proceeds, out-turn, income.
s.m. Message; mission, embassy; news, advice, intelligence ( = payām or paiyām)
subs: s.f. Messages to and fro, mutual messages, correspondence
s.m. 'Message-bearer,' messenger; ambassador, envoy; prophet, apostle
s.f. Mission (esp. of a prophet), legation; the office of a messenger, &c.

def: s.m. A prophet; apostle ( = paig̠ām-bar)
s.m. A ditch; a furrow.
s.m. Pegu (the country); — a sort of green coloured stone (brought from Pegu).
adv. Successively, one close upon another, close together, thick.
s.f. Solemn declaration, agreement, engagement, promise, vow; — vowed or sworn hostility or enmity
subs: v.n. To make a promise, or vow, &c.

def: s.f. (Old H.) = paiṭh, q.v.
s.f. = H پيجنا पजना paijnā, s.m. = paiṅjan, q.v.
v.n. = paij karnā, q.v.s.v. paij; — also = paiṭhnā, q.v.
s.m. = H پيجن पेजन pai-jan, s.f. = paiṅjan, q.v.
s.m. Messenger, runner; courier, an express; a runner before a tāziya (q.v.); a guard, watchman.
s.m.f. The head of an arrow, dart, javelin, &c.; any pointed missile.
s.m. pl. (of paik), Runners, couriers, &c.; armed militia or watchmen; — revenues of lands for the maintenance of paiks.
s.f. War, battle, contest (also written paigār).
s.m. = pāʼě-kār, q.v.s.v. pāʼě.

s.m.f. Face, countenance, visage; form, appearance, figure; resemblance, portrait, likeness; — (as last member of comp.), adj. Faced; — resembling, like (e.g. parī-paikar, 'fairy-faced'; koh-paikar, 'like a mountain').
s.m. See s.v. pai.
s.m. See s.v. pai.
s.m. See s.v. pai; and cf. pāʼe-ḵẖāna.
s.m. One who carries round a ḥuqqa for hire at a fair or in a camp; — a tumbler, mountebank; — s.f. Tumbling.
s.m. A vessel for measuring grain; a large basket.
adj. corr. of pahlā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (prov.), First; — first-born.
adv. (prov.), Beyond, across, over on the other side; removed (in time) by a whole unit
subs: The year before last; the year after next.

def: adv., corr. of pahle, q.v.s.v. pahlā.

ety: S. परिमा; or act. part. of paimūdan, 'to measure'
def: part. adj. & s.m. Measuring; weighing; — measurer, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. suḵẖun-paimā, 'a measurer of words, an orator').
s.f. = paimāʼish, q.v.
s.f. Measuring, surveying; measurement, measure, survey (of land)
subs: s.f. Settlement, measurement, a ḵẖasra survey
s.f. Trigonometrical, or revenue survey (by theodolite and compass)

def: s.f. Gold or silver lace or brocade (used for the border of a dress, or for a hat or cap).
s.m. Measuring; — agreement, compact, convention, treaty, stipulation, pledge, promise; security; confirmation; asseveration, oath.

ety: Zend paiti+rt. mā; S. prati+māṇ, rt. mā
def: s.m. A measure (for dry or wet goods); measure (of length, or capacity, &c.); a plane-scale (in land-measurement and mapping); a cup, bowl, goblet
subs: v.t. To fill up the measure (of one's) life, to kill; — v.n. The measure of one's days to be full, to die

ety: contrac. of paimūda, perf. part. of paimūdan; see paimāna
def: part. adj. Measured
s.f. = pāʼī, q.v.
s.m. Water-course, channel, drain; a rill; a reservoir of water.
adj. & s.m. Lower; back; — beneath, under; — lower part, back part, bottom: pāʼīn bāg̠, s.m. Garden at the back (of a palace or house).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Sharp; pointed; — a goad (for oxen, &c.)
s.m. A switch, a walking cane or stick ( = pāʼī).
s.m. Water-course, drain, gutter; spout (cf. pain).

ety: painā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. increment
def: v.t. To sharpen, give an edge to; to make pointed.
s.f. The tail of a peacock.
s.m. Return, requital, retaliation; a loan; repayment (of a loan.
v.t. To winnow; to sift ( = paćhoṛnā; phaṭaknā).
s.m. = palaiṅḍā; palhinḍā, q.v.
s.m. Road, way, path, track
subs: v.n. To stop a road; to rob on the highway.

def: s.m. Road, way, path, track
subs: v.n. To stop a road; to rob on the highway.

def: s.f. = palaiṅḍī, q.v.
s.f. Lowness; inferiority.
s.f. A goad.
s.f. An anklet of small bells.
s.f. = paiṅjan; — small bells fastened round the feet of children, and of pigeons; — paiṅjnī, s.f. A support for the axle-tree of a cart.
s.f. = paiṅjan; — small bells fastened round the feet of children, and of pigeons; — paiṅjnī, s.f. A support for the axle-tree of a cart.
s.m. = paikaṛā, q.v.s.v. pai; — the rope which fastens the two fore-legs of an animal together.
adj. Sixty-five.
adj. Forty-five.
adj. Forty-fifth.
s.m. Flourishing about before cudgelling, &c.; the preparatory attitudes or movements of a wrestler (also written paitarā)
subs: v.n. To go through the usual attitudes and flourishes before closing (with an antagonist).

def: s.f. = paiṭh, q.v.

ety: Prk. पायंतिआ or पयंतिआ; S. पाद+अन्तिका
def: s.f. The edge of the reservoir, where the man who receives the water-bag stands to throw up the water for irrigation.

ety: Prk. पणतीसा or पणतीसा; S. पञ्चत्रिंशत्
def: adj. Thirty-five.
adj. Thirty-fifth.

s.m. The foot; footstep; foot-print; the track of oxen (whether in treading out corn, or in working an oil-press, or in drawing water from a well, &c.); the place where corn is trodden out, the threshing floor; the place where corn is stacked; — corn in the straw; — pairoṅ, adv. On foot, afoot ( = paidal)
subs: v.n. To lift or raise the feet; to direct the steps (towards, -kī or), to proceed (in the direction of)
v.n. To continue menstruating after the usual time
pair rakhnā, v.n. To tread, to step.

def: s.m. = pair, q.v.
s.m. The track of oxen, &c. ( = pair, q.v.).
s.m. High wooden pattens (syn. khaṛāʼoṅ); — the track or path of oxen (in drawing water, &c. = pair, q.v.).

ety: act. part. of pairāstan; see next
def: adj. & s.m. (used as last member of compounds), adorning, decorating; adorned (with); adorner, decorator; one who executes (a thing) with elegance and accuracy.
s.m. Covering, mantle; a long robe; a kind of loose vest, a shirt or shift (resembling the qabā, but having buttons instead of strings at the neck and navel, and between the two).
s.m. contrac. of pairāhan, q.v.

ety: rt. of pairnā+Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका (तिका)
def: s.f. The act and the art of swimming ( = pairākī); place to be swum across; distance over which it is necessary to swim; — charge or wages for teaching to swim.
s.f. Adorning, decorating (used as the last member of compounds).

ety: rt. of pair-nā+Prk. आवको = S. आपकः (i.e. आपि caus. increment+अकः
def: s.m. A swimmer ( = tairāk).
s.f. The act of swimming; the art of swimming; — a female swimmer.
s.m. Circuit, circumference; environs, adjacent places; — the skirt, lappet, or flap of a garment; — adv. About, around.
s.m. Circuit, circumference; environs, adjacent places; — the skirt, lappet, or flap of a garment; — adv. About, around.

ety: caus. of pairnā; pairā = pair˚+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. increment
def: v.t. To cause to swim or float, to make (one) swim, to teach (one) to swim.

ety: rt. of pairnā+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: adj. Requiring to be swum across; beyond the depth of a man, unfordable, deep.

ety: perf. part. of pairāstan, fr. Zend vi+ā+rt. rād = S. rādh
def: part. adj. Decorated, adorned; trimmed; arranged, adjusted.
s.m. See s.v. pai.
s.f. See s.v. pai.
s.f. An ornament for the legs, an anklet; — the quantity of grain obtained after threshing.
s.f. Ladder, stair-case, flight of steps, stairs; step or rung of a ladder; — the track or path of oxen (in drawing water, &c. = paiṛā; pair, q.v.).

ety: pair˚ = Prk. पइर(इ) = S. प्रतिर(ति), rt. प्र+तॄ
def: v.n. To swim; to float.

ety: i.e. pāʼī or pāʼi+sā; S. पाद+इका+सदृशकः
def: s.m. A copper coin, a pice (the fourth part of an ānā; in value about a farthing and a half); — money, cash, wealth, riches, fortune
subs: (-kā), To spend money extravagantly; — to make away with the money (of another); to take the money (of another) by theft or deceit
v.n. To sink money, to lay out money without return
v.n. Money to be sunk or lost
(-kā), To spend extravagantly, to waste; — to subsist by wages or the produce (of labour); — to embezzle; to take bribes
(meṅ), To lay out or expend money (upon)
adj. & s.m. Moneyed, wealthy; — worth one paisā; — a moneyed man.

def: adj. & s.m. = paiśāć, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Relating or belonging to a piśāća or demon; infernal, demoniacal; — the eighth and lowest form of marriage (described as taking place when a lover secretly embraces a maiden either sleeping or intoxicated or disordered in her intellect); — a pisāća or demon.
s.f. A sort of gibberish put into the mouths of demons by the dramatic poets; one of the lowest forms of Prākr̤it dialects.
adj. Worth one paisa ( = paise-wālā).
s.m. Reach, compass, extent; access, entrance, admission; ability, power.
adj. = paiṅsaṭh, q.v.

ety: pais˚ = paʼis˚ = Prk. पइस(इ), or पविस(इ) = S. प्रविश(ति), rt. विश् with प्र
def: v.n. To enter, &c. = paiṭhnā, q.v.
s.m. Informing (against); tale-bearing, backbiting, calumny; espionage; malignity, depravity.
s.m. = paiśun, q.v.
adj. Worth one paisā ( = paise-wālā).
adj. (f. -ī), Shallow.
s.m. = paiṅtānā, q.v.
s.m. = paiṅtarā, q.v.
s.f. Entrance, entry, ingress, admission; penetration; impregnation; infiltration; — duplicate of a bill of exchange.

ety: caus. of paiṭhnā; ār or āl being the caus. increment
def: v.t. To cause to penetrate; to cause to pervade; to thrust (in, -meṅ), force (in); to run (in), to introduce (into); to insinuate.
v.t. = paiṭhālnā, q.v.

ety: rt. of paiṭhā-nā+āʼo = Prk. एअव्वअं = S. (इ)तव्य+कं
def: s.m. Penetration, entrance, entering, access, admission.

ety: caus. of paiṭhnā; ār or āl being the caus. increment
def: v.t. To cause to penetrate; to cause to pervade; to thrust (in, -meṅ), force (in); to run (in), to introduce (into); to insinuate.

ety: paiṭh˚ = Prk. पइट्ठ = S. प्रविष्ट perf. part. pass. of प्र+विश्;+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To enter (into, -meṅ), run or rush (in); to penetrate (into); to force an entry (into); to pervade; to intrude; to infiltrate
subs: (-ke andar), v.n. (intensive) To run or rush (into), &c. = paiṭhnā
v.t. To allow (one) to enter, to give entrance (to), to admit
s.m. One who enters, one who forces himself (in), an intruder; — a diver; a man who cleans out wells.

def: adj. Of or belonging to ancestors, or progenitors, or to the manes; paternal, ancestral, inherited from a father, hereditary
subs: s.f. A paternal estate; the country of one's ancestors, fatherland.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Pertaining to the bilious humour; proceeding from bile; bilious.
adj. Of a bilious temperament, bilious; biliary.

part. adj. (used in comp.), Joined, connected; joining, connecting, &c. ( = paiwasta); — s.m. Junction, conjunction, addition; connection, relation; relationship; — joint, join, piece, patch; graft; bud
subs: (-kā), To join (to), to make (a person or thing) a piece (of); to bury (the word zamīn being expressed, e.g. mujhe jītā-hī zamīn-kā paiwand kar-do)
(-meṅ), To put a piece (in or on); to add a piece (to), to patch; to graft; to inoculate (a tree)
(-kā), To be joined (to), to become a piece, part or parcel (of); to be interred (e.g. jab we zamīn-kā paiwand ho-jāweṅ).

def: adj. & s.m. Patched, pieced; grafted, engrafted; — an engrafted tree; — a hybrid, mongrel; a bastard; — a peach
subs: s.f. A patched covering or gabardine
s.f. Whiskers that join and are twisted in the beard in the form of a circle on the cheek (syn. gal-muććheṅ).

def: part. adj. Joined, &c. (see paiwasta; — s.f. Junction, conjunction, connection, &c. ( = paiwastagī, q.v.).

ety: part. of paiwastan; and = Zend paiti+basta; S. प्रतिबद्ध
def: part. adj. Joined, contiguous, adjacent, touching, sticking close, firmly united, inseparable; absorbed; successive, continued without interruption; — adv. Always, continually, uninterruptedly; — s.m. The year before last
subs: adj. & s.m. Having the eyebrows joined; — one whose eyebrows meet.

def: s.f. Junction, connection, union, adhesion, contiguity, closeness, coherence, continuity; close intimacy, attachment, friendship.
s.m. A wheel ( = pahiyā, q.v.).
s.m. See پيا paiyā; — an allowance of half an ānā on each rupee of revenue set apart for the paṭwārī or village accountant.
s.m. Autumn ( = pāyiz); (met.) old age, decline of life.
s.m. = pāʼeća, q.v.
s.f. A slipper; a shoe
subs: s.f. Fighting or mutual beating with slippers
s.f. The act of beating with a slipper
adj. 'Fit or deserving to receive a shoe-beating'; abject, mean, base
intj. It concerns my shoes! My shoes may care! I care not! (cf. balā-se)
v.n. To suffer a shoe-beating, be beaten with slippers.

def: v.n. To catch fire, to burn or blaze; to be burnt or consumed, become ashes; to be inflamed or incensed; to be in a burning fever.
